column88: All the camera trap data collected by the project I'm part of, are published online, on a data depository (GBIF). We found important to share data for possible reuse by other projects, as well as for transparency reasons. Data included various mountain species such as red deer, roe deer, fallow deer, wild boar, brown bear, red fox, chamois, etc.
column89: Regarding my previous answer, all the data was shared online, but we blurred their exact localisation. For all species, we blurred the localisation at a 400m grid cell. For two species, brown bear and capercaillie, we blurred the localisation at the mountain area level (centroid of a 800ha area). As those species are particularly sensitive (politically, in terms of their state of conservation, etc.), we found necessary to blur the exact location to prevent any disturbance by humans. If asked, we migh give the locations after discussing their use in detail with the person or project requesting them. For instance, I wouldn't give this information to someone who wants to go nature tourism or wildlife watching.
